name: DESCHUTES TITLE
address: 397 SW UPPER TERRACE DR, BEND, OR 97702

fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Landscaped Area
fr.considerations: Limited Side Access; Multiple Access Points
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the accessibility and visibility of access points on the property could significantly improve emergency response operations. Installing clear signage and ensuring pathways to these access points remain unobstructed may assist emergency personnel in quickly reaching the main building. Additionally, maintaining pedestrian walkways and parking areas free of clutter would support efficient movement and maneuverability for responders and their equipment. Adjusting landscaping near designated side access routes to minimize obstructions could further aid responder navigation and visibility. By implementing these recommendations, emergency response efforts may be more effective and timely, with responders able to locate and access critical areas swiftly.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure
wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space could be achieved by increasing the separation between vegetation and structures, particularly where vegetation is adjacent to the home. Removing or thinning the vegetation near the structure would help to reduce potential exposure and may decrease fuel continuity. In addition, managing the vegetated perimeter surrounding the property to create a wider buffer zone could enhance defensible space and contribute to long-term wildfire resilience. Consider increasing the clearance along the access route to support firefighter access and facilitate suppression efforts. Ensuring that vegetation along the driveway and other access areas is maintained can further enhance property preparedness, improving the avenue for emergency responders. Implementing regular vegetation management practices and expanding open spaces around the property could further strengthen the property's wildfire mitigation strategy.
